## HIGH IMPACT

### Component: `ha-sidebar` — Element names changed from `ha-md-list` / `ha-md-list-item` to `ha-list-nav` / `ha-list-item-button`

**What changed:** `src/components/ha-sidebar.ts` removes imports of `ha-md-list` and `ha-md-list-item` and replaces them with `ha-list-nav` and `ha-list-item-button`. The render template switches the wrapping list element from `<ha-md-list>` to `<ha-list-nav>`.

**What it breaks in ha-lcars:** The `card-mod-sidebar-yaml` block contains extensive CSS targeting `ha-md-list` and `ha-md-list-item` by element name throughout:

- `ha-md-list-item$` — top-level shadow-piercing rule for the whole sidebar item layout (icon columns, badge spans, selected state, configuration/notifications/user variants)
- `ha-md-list.ha-scrollbar` — scrollable list container margin, border-top, padding, font-family, height
- `ha-md-list-item` — border, background-color, width, margin, padding, border-radius, color for every nav item
- `ha-md-list-item.selected`, `.configuration`, `.notifications`, `.user` — all variant styles
- `ha-md-list.ha-scrollbar > .spacer::before` — spacer pseudo-element
- `ha-md-list:not(.ha-scrollbar)` — non-scrollable list padding and background
- The `@media screen and (max-width: 870px)` block references `.ha-scrollbar::before`
- `card-mod-sidebar-yaml: ha-md-list-item$` at the very top of the block targets the shadow DOM of each list item for `md-item` sub-element spacing

Additionally, in `card-mod-config-yaml`, the settings/info/navigation list blocks target `ha-md-list` and `ha-md-list-item` inside several config panel selectors:
- `ha-config-system-navigation $ ha-subpage ha-config-section ha-card ha-navigation-list $` → `ha-md-list` / `ha-md-list-item`
- `ha-config-dashboard $ ha-top-app-bar-fixed ha-config-section ha-card ha-config-navigation $ ha-navigation-list$` → `ha-md-list` / `ha-md-list-item`
- `ha-config-info $ hass-subpage div.content ha-card :is(.pages) ha-md-list` / `ha-md-list-item`
- The repairs and updates list blocks also target `ha-md-list` / `ha-md-list-item`

All of these selectors will now silently match nothing. The sidebar will render with HA default styling — no LCARS item color bars, no icon column treatment, no selected-state highlight, no badge positioning.

**Suggested fix:**

1. In `card-mod-sidebar-yaml`, replace every occurrence of `ha-md-list` with `ha-list-nav` and every occurrence of `ha-md-list-item` with `ha-list-item-button`. Audit the internal shadow structure of `ha-list-item-button` (it replaces `ha-md-list-item` which itself wrapped `md-item`) — the inner `md-item` selector in the `ha-md-list-item$` block may also need updating if `ha-list-item-button` uses a different shadow slot/part name.

2. In `card-mod-config-yaml`, replace `ha-md-list` → `ha-list-nav` and `ha-md-list-item` → `ha-list-item-button` in every config section block that styles navigation lists, repairs lists, and updates lists. CSS custom properties like `--md-list-item-label-text-color` and `--md-list-item-supporting-text-color` may also be renamed for the new component — verify against `ha-list-item-button`'s new variable API.

3. Verify that the class names `.ha-scrollbar`, `.panels-list`, `.wrapper`, `.spacer`, `.divider` are still present in `ha-list-nav`'s shadow DOM, as the `::before` pseudo-element and spacer rules depend on them.

---

### Component: `ha-top-app-bar-fixed` — Complete rewrite from MWC to plain LitElement; `ha-top-app-bar` removed entirely

**What changed:** `src/components/ha-top-app-bar-fixed.ts` is rewritten from scratch. It was previously a thin wrapper over `@material/mwc-top-app-bar-fixed/mwc-top-app-bar-fixed-base`. It is now a native LitElement with its own shadow DOM structure using plain HTML elements. `src/components/ha-top-app-bar.ts` is **deleted** entirely (the file goes from 37 lines to 0 — the diff shows `+0 -37`).

**What it breaks in ha-lcars:**

1. **`--mdc-*` variables on the app bar no longer apply.** The old MWC component consumed `--mdc-top-app-bar-width`, `--mdc-typography-headline6-font-weight`, `--mdc-top-app-bar-fixed-box-shadow`, etc. The new component has its own CSS that uses `--app-header-background-color`, `--app-header-text-color`, and a new internal `.top-app-bar` class. The LCARS `&base` block sets `app-header-background-color` and `app-header-text-color` which are preserved, but any MWC-specific overrides in card-mod CSS that targeted `.mdc-top-app-bar`, `.mdc-top-app-bar__row`, or `.mdc-top-app-bar--fixed-adjust` will match nothing.

2. **`home-assistant-main.ts` removes `--mdc-drawer-width` and `--mdc-top-app-bar-width`** and replaces them with new `--ha-si…` variables (the diff is truncated but shows the old variables being dropped). The LCARS sidebar `card-mod-sidebar-yaml` references `var(--mdc-drawer-width,256px)` in the mobile `@media` block:
   ```css
   width: calc( var(--mdc-drawer-width,256px) - 61px );
   ```
   This will fall back to `256px` which may be incorrect if the new variable has a different value.

3. **`card-mod-config-yaml` targets `ha-config-dashboard $ ha-top-app-bar-fixed ha-config-section`** — this selector chain traverses into the shadow DOM of `ha-top-app-bar-fixed`. If the new component renders its content in a different slot or wrapper element, this selector may no longer pierce to `ha-config-section` correctly.

4. **`ha-top-app-bar` element is gone.** If any card-mod rule or selector in the theme references `ha-top-app-bar` (the non-fixed variant), it will silently fail.

**Suggested fixes:**

- In `card-mod-sidebar-yaml` mobile `@media` block: change `var(--mdc-drawer-width,256px)` to the new variable name (determine from the truncated `home-assistant-main.ts` diff — it begins `--ha-si…`, likely `--ha-sidebar-width`).
- Audit the new `ha-top-app-bar-fixed` shadow DOM structure: the new component uses a `.top-app-bar` class div. Any card-mod rules that pierce into it should be updated to match `.top-app-bar` rather than `.mdc-top-app-bar` or `.mdc-top-app-bar__row`.
- Remove or update any selector referencing `ha-top-app-bar` (non-fixed) since the element no longer exists.
- Verify `--app-header-background-color` and `--app-header-text-color` are still consumed by the new component (they appear in the new component's CSS in the diff, so these LCARS variables remain effective).

---

### Component: `home-assistant-main` — `--mdc-drawer-width` and `--mdc-top-app-bar-width` removed; drawer event renamed

**What changed:** `src/layouts/home-assistant-main.ts` removes `--mdc-drawer-width` and `--mdc-top-app-bar-width` CSS custom properties and replaces them with `--ha-si…` (truncated in diff, but the removal is explicit). The drawer closed event is renamed from `@MDCDrawer:closed` to `@hass-drawer-closed`.

**What it breaks in ha-lcars:**

The LCARS sidebar `card-mod-sidebar-yaml` contains this in the mobile `@media` block:
```css
width: calc( var(--mdc-drawer-width,256px) - 61px );
```
The variable `--mdc-drawer-width` is no longer set by HA. The fallback of `256px` may not match the actual sidebar width in the new implementation, causing the mobile `::before` elbow pseudo-element to be incorrectly sized.

**Suggested fix:** Replace `var(--mdc-drawer-width,256px)` with `var(--ha-sidebar-width, 256px)` (or whatever the complete new variable name resolves to from the truncated diff). Inspect the new `home-assistant-main.ts` styles to confirm the exact variable name (`--ha-si…`).

---

## MEDIUM IMPACT

### Component: `ha-sidebar` — Internal class structure and scroll behavior changes

**What changed:** The switch from `ha-md-list` to `ha-list-nav` likely brings different internal CSS class names for the scroll container, spacer, and divider elements. The LCARS theme targets `.panels-list > .wrapper::before`, `.spacer`, `.divider`, and `.ha-scrollbar` — these may or may not be preserved in `ha-list-nav`.

**What it affects:** The vertical border line drawn alongside the nav items (`.panels-list > .wrapper::before`), the bottom spacer pseudo-element (`ha-md-list.ha-scrollbar > .spacer::before`), and the hidden divider (`.divider { display: none }`). If these class names changed, the visual structure of the LCARS sidebar elbow and spacing will be broken or partially visible.

**Suggested fix:** Inspect the shadow DOM of the new `ha-list-nav` component at runtime to determine current class names. Update the LCARS `card-mod-sidebar-yaml` selectors for `.panels-list`, `.wrapper`, `.spacer`, and `.divider` accordingly.

---

### Component: `ha-top-app-bar-fixed` — New internal layout classes; `div.header` toolbar selectors may be affected

**What changed:** The new `ha-top-app-bar-fixed` renders a `.top-app-bar` div with action slot areas. The `card-mod-root-yaml` block targets `div.header` and its `.toolbar` child for the LCARS elbow, clock pseudo-element, and sidebar toggle. These rules pierce from the root context into the header element. If the new component's slot/part structure changes where `.toolbar` lives in the composed tree, these rules may stop applying.

**What it affects:**
- `div.header .toolbar { ... }` — the entire header styling including the LCARS elbow `::before`, clock `::after`, and the border-right that creates the LCARS frame
- `div.header .toolbar ha-menu-button` — sidebar toggle visibility
- `div.header .toolbar &::before` / `&::after` — the elbow shadow and clock display

**Suggested fix:** After upgrading, verify in the browser that the clock and elbow still render. If not, inspect whether `div.header` and `.toolbar` are still present in the composed DOM tree, or if the new component uses different wrapper element names.

---

### Component: `ha-icon-button` — `ha-svg-icon` inside now has `aria-hidden="true"`

**What changed:** `src/components/ha-icon-button.ts` now adds `aria-hidden="true"` to the inner `<ha-svg-icon>` when rendering from a path. This is a DOM attribute change on the child element.

**What it affects:** Any LCARS card-mod CSS that uses an attribute selector like `ha-svg-icon:not([aria-hidden])` or targets `ha-svg-icon` inside icon buttons with attribute conditions would behave differently. The LCARS theme does not appear to use such selectors currently, but icon button icon styling (color, size) via `ha-icon-button ha-svg-icon` selectors is unaffected.

**Suggested fix:** No immediate fix needed, but be aware if adding future selectors that target `ha-svg-icon` inside icon buttons — the attribute is now always present.

---

### Component: `ha-state-icon` — Refactored to use context API instead of `hass` property

**What changed:** `src/components/ha-state-icon.ts` drops the `hass` property in favor of consuming `configContext`, `connectionContext`, and `entitiesContext` via `@lit/context`. The component still renders the same icon, but its data source changes.

**What it affects:** The LCARS theme targets `ha-state-icon` in the button card classes:
```css
> ha-state-icon {
  --mdc-icon-size: unset;
  background-color: var(--lcars-card-top-color);
  ...
}
```
The visual appearance of the icon itself should be unaffected since the element name and rendered output are the same. However, if any other LCARS logic (outside the diff scope) passed `hass` to `ha-state-icon` as a property, that would now be ignored.

**Suggested fix:** No CSS fix needed. The existing `ha-state-icon` selectors remain valid.

---

### Component: `hass-tabs-subpage-data-table` — `advancedOnly` navigation property removed

**What changed:** `src/layouts/hass-tabs-subpage.ts` removes the `advancedOnly` property from `PageNavigation`. `hass-tabs-subpage-data-table.ts` removes the `userData?.showAdvanced` check from `changedProperties` comparison.

**What it affects:** The LCARS theme targets `hass-tabs-subpage $` in many config panel selectors for the `div.bottom-bar` height override. These selectors themselves are not broken. However, if the theme previously relied on advanced-mode-only pages being hidden (affecting tab counts and the `showTabs` visibility), the tab bar behavior may change slightly — tabs may now always show regardless of advanced mode setting.

**Suggested fix:** No CSS fix needed. Monitor whether any previously hidden tabs now appear and affect the bottom bar layout.

---

## LOW IMPACT

### Component: `ha-drawer` — Migrated to Web Awesome drawer; event name changed

**What changed:** Per `home-assistant-main.ts`, the drawer closed event was renamed from `MDCDrawer:closed` to `hass-drawer-closed`. The `ha-drawer` component was previously migrated to Web Awesome (PR #51990 in this release list).

**What it affects in ha-lcars:** The `card-mod-drawer` block in `preamble.yaml` styles the `:host` of the drawer for texture and gradient effects. These `:host` pseudo-element rules (`::before` and `::after`) should be unaffected by the event rename. No CSS selector change needed.

**Suggested fix:** No fix needed for the current card-mod-drawer texture/gradient rules. Monitor if the Web Awesome `<wa-drawer>` shadow DOM changes how `:host` pseudo-elements render (blending may differ if the shadow root changes stacking context).

---

### Component: New list components — `ha-list-nav`, `ha-list-item-button`

**What changed:** PR #51705 "New list components" and subsequent migration PRs introduce `ha-list-nav` and `ha-list-item-button` as replacements for `ha-md-list` and `ha-md-list-item` in multiple places across the UI (sidebar already noted above as HIGH).

**What it affects in ha-lcars:** Beyond the sidebar (HIGH), other config panels may progressively migrate to the new list components. The existing `ha-md-list` / `ha-md-list-item` selectors in `card-mod-config-yaml` for settings navigation, repairs, updates, and info pages should be audited as these panels may also be migrated.

**Suggested fix:** Track future releases for additional `ha-md-list` → `ha-list-nav` migrations in config panels. The new components likely use different CSS custom property names than `--md-list-item-label-text-color` and `--md-list-item-supporting-text-color`.

---
